title: Prefer compiler output unless hand-asm is justified
description: Write assembly by hand only for a measured, specific need the compiler cannot meet, keeping it small, isolated, and backed by a high-level fallback.
body: Use when tempted to write a routine in assembly. Do not hand-write code the compiler already generates well, and do not scatter assembly through the codebase. Actions: confirm a concrete reason (a specific instruction with no intrinsic, an ABI shim, a precise timing/atomic sequence), keep the asm in a small isolated function, prefer intrinsics over raw asm where possible, and keep a portable high-level fallback. Validation signals include a measured benefit over compiler output and the asm function passing the same tests as the fallback. Risk boundary: hand-written assembly is unportable and hard to maintain, and the compiler often beats it; if there is no measured, specific justification, do not introduce it.
